pattern: A piece can only be as long as the word minus one character reserved for every other friend, but the box also holds every shorter piece anchored at the very end -- so the winner is either the longest piece anchored near the front or one of the many end-anchored pieces of every length.
hints: No piece can be longer than L = len(word) - numFriends + 1, because every other friend still needs at least one character.

A piece that touches neither end of the word needs two other friends to absorb both sides, so those only exist once numFriends is large enough -- but a piece that runs all the way to the end of the word only ever needs friends on its left, so every length from 1 up to L anchored at the end is always reachable.

Collect the length-L substring starting at each of the first numFriends-1 positions, plus every suffix of the word starting at position numFriends-1 through the last character, and return the lexicographically largest string among all of them.

edge_inputs.description: every friend gets one repeated letter
single friend takes the whole word
as many friends as letters, one char each
shared prefix forces looking past the first character
all identical letters, ties broken by any valid start
a later start beats an earlier one once characters differ

stress.description: long random lowercase word with a random friend count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    word = ''.join(rng.choice('abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz') for _ in range(n))
    numFriends = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [word, numFriends]
stress.note: numFriends is always between 1 and the word length

complexity.time: O(n^2) worst case for the candidate comparisons
